title: 1.1: Explains that switching buffers changes a pointer to the active flag array without copying data between buffers.
1.2: Reports the stack size limit (e.g., 8 entries per buffer).
1.3: Describes stack exhaustion behavior when limit is exceeded (e.g., oldest entry silently evicted, no error raised).
1.4: Shows that Ctrl+Shift+a produces CSI u format (e.g., \\x1b[97;6u) even when no keyboard flags are set (flags=0).
1.6: States the modifier bit values: shift=1, alt=2, ctrl=4.
1.7: States that the +1 offset is always added to the sum of modifier bits in CSI u format (e.g., the transmitted value is m+1, not just m).
1.8: Identifies that screen reset is a condition where both buffer stacks are cleared simultaneously.
1.9: States that there is no state leakage or unexpected interaction when rapidly switching between buffers while manipulating keyboard mode.
2.1: Claims that Ctrl+A (without Shift) produces a shifted_key suffix :65 in the output (e.g., states \\x1b[97:65;5u).
